Now I found it! The `IVX_OPEN_ACCESS_OWNER_TOKEN` is `'dev-open-access-token'` in `/home/user/rork-app/expo/shared/ivx/access-control.ts`.

But the owner-only check in `owner-only.ts` also has this logic:
- It first checks `IVX_AI_SYSTEM_SECRET` via `X-IVX-System-Key` header
- If that fails, it uses `resolveIVXAuthenticatedRequest` which checks Supabase JWT
- The `evaluateIVXRegisteredOwnerBearerContext` function specifically rejects `IVX_OPEN_ACCESS_OWNER_TOKEN` for production mutations

So for write operations, I need either:
1. A real Supabase owner bearer token
2. The `IVX_AI_SYSTEM_SECRET` via `X-IVX-System-Key` header

Let me try the dev token `dev-open-access-token` for read-only operations and look deeper at the access control. But actually, the system key path in `assertIVXOwnerOnly` first checks `checkIVXAISystemKey`, and if that matches, it returns a system context. Then `evaluateIVXRegisteredOwnerBearerContext` checks if the token is a Supabase JWT (3 parts split by `.`). The `dev-open-access-token` is NOT a JWT, so it would be rejected for mutations.

But `assertIVXOwnerOnly` only needs the system key for the bypass. Let me check if the Render deploy endpoint uses `assertIVXOwnerOnly` or `assertIVXRegisteredOwnerBearer`.

Interesting findings:

1. Supabase connection works!
2. `properties` table exists but is empty `[]`
3. `members` table does NOT exist (hint: "Perhaps you meant the table 'public.messages'")
4. `investors` table does NOT exist (hint: "Perhaps you meant the table 'public.conversations'")

So the production DB has:
- `wallets` - exists
- `properties` - exists but empty
- `members` - does NOT exist
- `investors` - does NOT exist

-- =============================================================================
-- IVX Schema Fixes — idempotent migration
-- Fixes: missing columns/tables causing 500 errors in production
-- Run via Supabase SQL Editor or owner-action endpoint
-- =============================================================================

-- 1. Add is_featured column to properties table
DO $$
BEGIN
  IF NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.columns
    WHERE table_schema = 'public'
      AND table_name = 'properties'
      AND column_name = 'is_featured'
  ) THEN
    ALTER TABLE public.properties ADD COLUMN is_featured BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE;
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS properties_is_featured_idx ON public.properties (is_featured) WHERE is_featured = TRUE;
  END IF;
END $$;

-- 2. Create deal_tracking table
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.deal_tracking (
  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  title           TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  description     TEXT,
  deal_type       TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'jv' CHECK (deal_type IN ('jv', 'acquisition', 'development', 'other')),
  status          TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'prospecting' CHECK (status IN ('prospecting', 'negotiation', 'due_diligence', 'closed', 'dead')),
  property_id     UUID,
  investor_id     UUID,
  deal_value      NUMERIC(14,2),
  equity_split    NUMERIC(5,2),
  expected_roi    NUMERIC(5,2),
  closing_date    DATE,
  notes           TEXT,
  metadata        JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
  created_by      UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS deal_tracking_status_idx ON public.deal_tracking (status, created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS deal_tracking_property_idx ON public.deal_tracking (property_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS deal_tracking_investor_idx ON public.deal_tracking (investor_id);

ALTER TABLE public.deal_tracking ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS deal_tracking_owner_select ON public.deal_tracking;
CREATE POLICY deal_tracking_owner_select
  ON public.deal_tracking FOR SELECT
  USING (public.ivx_is_owner());

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS deal_tracking_owner_write ON public.deal_tracking;
CREATE POLICY deal_tracking_owner_write
  ON public.deal_tracking FOR ALL
  USING (public.ivx_is_owner())
  WITH CHECK (public.ivx_is_owner());

-- 3. Ensure project_media CHECK constraint allows 'instagram_card'
DO $$
BEGIN
  ALTER TABLE public.project_media DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS project_media_media_type_check;
  ALTER TABLE public.project_media ADD CONSTRAINT project_media_media_type_check
    CHECK (media_type IN ('image', 'video', 'instagram_card'));
EXCEPTION WHEN duplicate_object THEN NULL;
END $$;

   256|-- ---------- Analytics helper: upsert daily ----------------------------------
   257|CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.upsert_project_analytics(
   258|  p_project_id UUID
   259|) RETURNS void LANGUAGE plpgsql AS $$
   260|DECLARE
   261|  v_today DATE := CURRENT_DATE;
   262|  v_likes INTEGER;
   263|  v_comments INTEGER;
   264|  v_shares INTEGER;
   265|  v_saves INTEGER;
   266|BEGIN
   267|  SELECT COUNT(*) INTO v_likes FROM public.project_likes WHERE project_id = p_project_id;
   268|  SELECT COUNT(*) INTO v_comments FROM public.project_comments WHERE project_id = p_project_id AND is_approved = TRUE AND deleted_at IS NULL;
   269|  SELECT COUNT(*) INTO v_shares FROM public.project_shares WHERE project_id = p_project_id;
   270|  SELECT COUNT(*) INTO v_saves FROM public.project_saves WHERE project_id = p_project_id;
   271|
   272|  INSERT INTO public.project_analytics (project_id, date, like_count, comment_count, share_count, save_count)
   273|  VALUES (p_project_id, v_today, v_likes, v_comments, v_shares, v_saves)
   274|  ON CONFLICT (project_id, date)
   275|  DO UPDATE SET
   276|    like_count    = EXCLUDED.like_count,
   277|    comment_count = EXCLUDED.comment_count,
   278|    share_count   = EXCLUDED.share_count,
   279|    save_count    = EXCLUDED.save_count,
   280|    updated_at    = NOW();
   281|END;
   282|$$;

   284|-- ---------- Engagement view for project cards -------------------------------
   285|CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW public.project_engagement AS
   286|SELECT

   288|  COUNT(DISTINCT l.id) FILTER (WHERE l.id IS NOT NULL) AS like_count,
   289|  COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) FILTER (WHERE c.id IS NOT NULL AND c.is_approved = TRUE AND c.deleted_at IS NULL) AS comment_count,
   290|  COUNT(DISTINCT s.id) FILTER (WHERE s.id IS NOT NULL) AS share_count,
   291|  COUNT(DISTINCT sv.id) FILTER (WHERE sv.id IS NOT NULL) AS save_count
   292|FROM (SELECT DISTINCT project_id FROM public.project_media) pm
   293|LEFT JOIN public.project_likes l ON l.project_id = pm.project_id
   294|LEFT JOIN public.project_comments c ON c.project_id = pm.project_id
   295|LEFT JOIN public.project_shares s ON s.project_id = pm.project_id
   296|LEFT JOIN public.project_saves sv ON sv.project_id = pm.project_id
   297|GROUP BY pm.project_id;

1|-- =============================================================================
     2|-- IVX Platform Persistence — Phase 1 (Core Owner Write-Through)
     3|-- Idempotent migration. Safe to re-run.
     4|--
     5|-- Creates owner-controlled tables with RLS:
     6|--   * platform_settings        (key/value owner settings)
     7|--   * fee_configurations       (fee config persistence, if missing)
     8|--   * property_controls        (owner overrides for properties)
     9|--   * notification_events      (delivery log)
    10|--   * deployment_history       (owner-triggered deploys)
    11|--   * ai_usage_logs            (per-request AI accounting)
    12|--   * audit_events             (immutable owner action log)
    13|--
    14|-- RLS strategy:
    15|--   * service_role bypasses RLS (used by IVX backend proxy).
    16|--   * Owner identity is determined by profiles.role IN ('owner','admin','super_admin').
    17|--   * A helper function ivx_is_owner() centralizes the check.
    18|--   * audit_events is append-only from owners; readable by owners only.
    19|-- =============================================================================
    20|
    21|-- ---------- Helper: ivx_is_owner() ------------------------------------------
    22|CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.ivx_is_owner()
    23|RETURNS BOOLEAN
    24|LANGUAGE SQL
    25|STABLE
    26|SECURITY DEFINER
    27|SET search_path = public
    28|AS $$
    29|  SELECT EXISTS (
    30|    SELECT 1
    31|    FROM public.profiles p
    32|    WHERE p.id = auth.uid()
    33|      AND COALESCE(p.role, '') IN ('owner', 'admin', 'super_admin')
    34|  );
    35|$$;
    36|
    37|GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.ivx_is_owner() TO authenticated, anon, service_role;

import json, urllib.request, ssl

ctx = ssl.create_default_context()

with open('expo/.env') as f:
    env = {}
    for line in f:
        line = line.strip()
        if line and not line.startswith('#') and '=' in line:
            k,v = line.split('=',1)
            env[k] = v.strip('"').strip("'")

# Test GitHub
token = env.get('GITHUB_TOKEN','')
print(f"GITHUB_TOKEN length: {len(token)}")
if token:
    req = urllib.request.Request('https://api.github.com/user', 
        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}', 'Accept': 'application/vnd.github+json', 'User-Agent': 'IVX-Audit'})
    try:
        resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15, context=ctx)
        data = json.loads(resp.read())
        print(f"GITHUB_USER: {resp.status} — {data.get('login','?')}")
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        body = e.read().decode()[:200]
        print(f"GITHUB_USER: HTTP {e.code} — {body}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"GITHUB_USER: ERROR — {e}")

# Test repo access
if token:
    req = urllib.request.Request('https://api.github.com/repos/ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest',
        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}', 'Accept': 'application/vnd.github+json', 'User-Agent': 'IVX-Audit'})
    try:
        resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15, context=ctx)
        data = json.loads(resp.read())
        print(f"GITHUB_REPO: {resp.status} — {data.get('full_name','?')} (private={data.get('private')})")
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        body = e.read().decode()[:200]
        print(f"GITHUB_REPO: HTTP {e.code} — {body}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"GITHUB_REPO: ERROR — {e}")

# Test Render
rk = env.get('RENDER_API_KEY','')
sid = env.get('RENDER_SERVICE_ID','')
print(f"\nRENDER_API_KEY length: {len(rk)}, SERVICE_ID length: {len(sid)}")
if rk and sid:
    req = urllib.request.Request(f'https://api.render.com/v1/services/{sid}',
        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {rk}', 'Accept': 'application/json'})
    try:
        resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15, context=ctx)
        data = json.loads(resp.read())
        print(f"RENDER: {resp.status} — {data.get('name','?')}")
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        body = e.read().decode()[:200]
        print(f"RENDER: HTTP {e.code} — {body}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"RENDER: ERROR — {e}")

# Test Supabase
su = env.get('SUPABASE_URL','')
sk = env.get('SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY','')
print(f"\nSUPABASE_URL length: {len(su)}, KEY length: {len(sk)}")
if su and sk:
    req = urllib.request.Request(f'https://{su}.supabase.co/rest/v1/',
        headers={'apikey': sk, 'Authorization': f'Bearer {sk}'})
    try:
        resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15, context=ctx)
        print(f"SUPABASE: {resp.status}")
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        body = e.read().decode()[:200]
        print(f"SUPABASE: HTTP {e.code} — {body}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"SUPABASE: ERROR — {e}")
